rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory section to support a key mechanism in the citing paper: increasing commitment as consumers get closer to completing a goal. The authors explicitly discuss Kivetz, Urminsky, and Zheng (2006) when motivating their completion-utility component and its goal-gradient logic, which feeds into hypothesis development about abandonment over time. This is more than a passing background mention, even though the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's data or methods.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Kivetz et al. (2006) as conceptual support for goal-gradient effects and uses that logic to build its completion-commitment term in a waiting-abandonment model. It incorporates this prior theory into its own framing of why persistence can increase as the end of a wait appears near.
